Package algatorgraph

Class GraphCreator

  • java.lang.Object
    • algatorgraph.GraphCreator
  • public class GraphCreator
extends java.lang.Object
    GraphCreator is a utility class for generating and importing various types of graphs using the JGraphT library and other classes and methods, that produce JGraphT graphs. It supports generating predefined graph types (named graphs, random graphs, structured graphs), importing graphs from various file formats, creating visual representations of graphs and creating the generation of the html website showing all possible graph generations. The class serves as a unified interface for graph creation and manipulation in the ALGator system.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class Description
      static class  GraphCreator.GraphWebsiteGenerator
      Inner class for generating HTML documentation website showcasing all available graphs.

        Returns a random graph from the selected collection.

      • importGraphFromFile

        public static org.jgrapht.Graph importGraphFromFile​(java.io.File file)
        Imports a single graph from a file. Automatically detects format based on extension and handles critical errors like OutOfMemory. * @param file The file to be parsed.
        Returns:
        A Graph object or null if parsing fails

      • importGraphsFromFolder

        public static java.util.List<org.jgrapht.Graph> importGraphsFromFolder​(java.lang.String folderPath)
        Public method used to import graphs from a folder containing various graph file formats. It automatically detects file formats, uses appropriate parsers, returns a list of generated graphs and creates a import_log.txt file containing a log of import successes and failures. Usage example: List<Graph> importedGraphs = GraphCreator.importGraphsFromFolder("path_to_folder");
        Parameters:
        folderPath - Path to directory containing graph files
        Returns:
        List of imported graphs as JGraphT Graph objects

      • parseG6

        public static org.jgrapht.Graph<java.lang.String,​org.jgrapht.graph.DefaultEdge> parseG6​(java.io.File file)
        Parses a .g6 or .s6 format file (graph6/sparse6 format) and creates a graph.
        Parameters:
        file - .g6/.s6 file to parse
        Returns:
        A graph or null if parsing fails

      • generateGraph

        public static org.jgrapht.Graph<java.lang.Integer,​org.jgrapht.graph.DefaultWeightedEdge> generateGraph​(java.lang.String[] args)
        Generates a JGraphT graph instance, based on the given arguments. The graph type is determined by the first element in the args array, which must match one of the enumerated GraphCreator.GraphTypes values. Subsequent elements are interpreted as type-specific parameters (e.g., number of vertices, edges, probabilities, etc.). Supported graph types include both named graphs (Petersen, Karate Club, etc.) and parameterized generators (Complete, Path, Scale-Free, etc.). Example usage 1: Complete graph String[] argsComplete = {"COMPLETE", "5"}; Graph<Integer, DefaultEdge> completeGraph = generateGraph(argsComplete); Example usage 2: Directed scale-free graph String[] argsDirected = {"DIRECTED_SCALE_FREE", "0.3", "0.5", "0.1", "0.1", "50", "20"}; Graph<Integer, DefaultEdge> directedScaleFreeGraph = generateGraph(argsDirected);
        Parameters:
        args - Arguments specifying the graph type and its parameters:
        • [0] - Graph type (from GraphCreator.GraphTypes enum)
        • [1..n] - Type-specific parameters (getGraphDescription(java.lang.String,java.lang.String,java.lang.String) for details)
        Returns:
        A Graph of type Graph<Integer, DefaultEdge> representing the generated graph, or null if the arguments are invalid.

      • getGraphDescription

        public static java.lang.String getGraphDescription​(java.lang.String graphName)
        Provides descriptive information about each graph type we can generate with the generateGraph method. Example usage: String description = getGraphDescription("BIDIAKIS_CUBE"); System.out.println(description); Output: "Bidakis cube graph - a 3-regular graph with 12 vertices and 18 edges. Parameters: []"
        Parameters:
        graphName - Name of the graph type (must match enum values)
        Returns:
        User-friendly descriptions with parameter explanations
